title: fix(mcp): security scoping for skills and code-review
rationale: linked to issue #1777, security topic, multi-round reviews
body:
Closes #1777

Follow-up from PR #1775 findings:
- [x] server/mcp/tools/skills.ts: Added org-scoped task access check in `recommend_skills`
- [x] server/mcp/tools/code-review.ts: Fixed `org_id` vs null `sessionOrg` collision risk
- [x] Added tests for both fixes in `test/mcp/skills.test.ts` and `test/mcp/code-review.test.ts`

botReviews.body:
## Code Review — PR #1781

**fix(mcp): security scoping for skills and code-review**

4 files changed · 139 additions · 6 deletions · 5 findings (1 blocker, 3 major, 1 minor)

---

### BLOCKER

**`server/mcp/tools/skills.ts:320` — Null orgId bypasses org-scoping check (fail-open)**

The guard `if (orgId && task.organizationId !== orgId)` short-circuits when `orgId` is null, allowing a session with no org context to read skill recommendations for **any** task regardless of ownership. Every other tool file (code-review.ts, epics.ts, kg.ts, stack.ts) blocks when the session org is null. The code-review.ts handlers in this same PR use `!sessionOrg ||` (fail-closed), making this permissive path inconsistent and dangerous.

```ts
// Current — fail-open
if (orgId && task.organizationId !== orgId) {

// Should be — fail-closed, matching code-review.ts
if (!orgId || task.organizationId !== orgId) {
```

---

### MAJOR

**`server/mcp/tools/skills.ts:321` — Forbidden response missing `isError: true`**

Uses `jsonResponse({ error: "forbidden", message: "..." })` which does **not** set `isError: true` on the MCP ToolResponse. Every other tool file uses `errorResponse()` for authorization errors (39 call sites across code-review.ts, epics.ts, kg.ts, stack.ts). An MCP client checking `response.isError` will silently treat this forbidden response as a successful call.

```ts
// Current — no isError flag
return jsonResponse({ error: "forbidden", message: "Task does not belong to your organization" });

// Should be
return errorResponse('forbidden', 'Task does not belong to your organization');
```

**`test/mcp/skills.test.ts` — No test for null-orgId permissive path**

The three existing tests cover same-org success, different-org forbidden, and not-found. The most security-critical case — `getCurrentOrgId()` returning `null` (the condition that bypasses the guard) — has zero test coverage. A fourth test should mock null orgId and verify access is denied.

**`test/mcp/code-review.test.ts:732` — Only 1 of 3 handlers tested for null sessionOrg**

The null-sessionOrg guard was applied to three handlers (`record_review_started`, `record_finding`, `record_review_completed`) but the new test block only covers `record_review_started`. Add test cases for the other two handlers to prevent inconsistent regressions.

---

### MINOR

**`server/mcp/tools/skills.ts:309` — Unused `userId` variable shadows outer scope**

`const userId = getCurrentUserId() || "anonymous"` is declared but never used in the `recommend_skills` case block. The outer `handle()` function already resolves these at lines 124-125. Remove the redundant declarations.

---

Reviewed SHA: 25bb2373202f4a0583cc9ef76023ea7e879d7a17

## Code Review — aictrl-dev/aictrl#1781

**PR:** fix(mcp): security scoping for skills and code-review
**Files changed:** 4 | **+140 / -6**

---

### BLOCKER

| # | File | Line | Summary |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 1 | `server/mcp/tools/skills.ts` | 320 | Org tenant guard skipped when session has no orgId |

**#1 — Org tenant guard skipped when session has no orgId**
The condition `if (orgId && task.organizationId !== orgId)` only enforces the org boundary when `orgId` is truthy. If the session has no org (unauthenticated or personal workspace), the guard is skipped entirely, allowing a session-less caller to access tasks belonging to any organization. This is inconsistent with the fix in `code-review.ts` which correctly uses `if (!sessionOrg || a.org_id !== sessionOrg)` to reject all unscoped sessions.

```typescript
// Current (vulnerable):
if (orgId && task.organizationId !== orgId) {
  return jsonResponse({ error: "forbidden", message: "..." });
}

// Should be (consistent with code-review.ts):
if (!orgId || task.organizationId !== orgId) {
  return errorResponse('forbidden', 'No organization context or task does not belong to your organization');
}
```

---

### MAJOR

| # | File | Line | Summary |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 2 | `server/mcp/tools/skills.ts` | 321 | Security error uses `jsonResponse` instead of `errorResponse` |

**#2 — Security error uses `jsonResponse` instead of `errorResponse`**
The new forbidden guard in `recommend_skills` returns `jsonResponse({ error: 'forbidden' })`, which does NOT set `isError: true` on the ToolResponse. Every other MCP tool (`code-review.ts`, `epics.ts`, `kg.ts`, `stack.ts`) uses `errorResponse()` for security violations, which sets `isError: true`. Clients that check `isError` to detect auth failures will silently miss this forbidden case. The test at `skills.test.ts:79` even asserts `expect(result.isError).toBeUndefined()`, codifying the inconsistency.

```typescript
// Current:
return jsonResponse({ error: "forbidden", message: "Task does not belong to your organization" });

// Should be:
return errorResponse('forbidden', 'Task does not belong to your organization');
```

---

### MINOR

| # | File | Line | Summary |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 3 | `server/mcp/tools/skills.ts` | 309 | Redundant variable declarations shadow outer scope |
| 4 | `server/mcp/tools/code-review.ts` | 408–552 | Identical null-org guard duplicated 3 times |
| 5 | `test/mcp/code-review.test.ts` | 731 | Only 1 of 3 null-org guards is tested |
| 6 | `test/mcp/skills.test.ts` | 76 | No test for null/undefined orgId in recommend_skills |

**#3 — Redundant variable declarations shadow outer scope**
`userId` and `orgId` at lines 309–310 are already declared at lines 124–125 in the enclosing function scope. The re-declarations shadow them with slightly different null-coalescing logic (`|| 'anonymous'` vs `?? undefined` for the outer `orgId`). This could cause subtle differences if the outer declarations change.

**#4 — Identical null-org guard duplicated 3 times in one file**
The pattern `const sessionOrg = getCurrentOrgId(); if (!sessionOrg || a.org_id !== sessionOrg) { return errorResponse(...) }` is copy-pasted at lines ~408, ~471, and ~549. Consider extracting a shared helper (e.g., `requireOrgMatch(claimedOrg)`) to avoid future drift.

**#5 — Only 1 of 3 null-org guards is tested**
The PR adds identical null-sessionOrg guards to three sub-commands (`record_review_started`, `record_finding`, `complete_review`) but only tests `record_review_started`. The other two have the same guard but no test. Add analogous test cases or parameterise with `describe.each`.

**#6 — No test for null/undefined orgId in recommend_skills**
The code-review test file covers the null-sessionOrg case explicitly, but the new skills test file has no analogous test for when `getCurrentOrgId()` returns null. Given the guard uses `if (orgId && ...)` (short-circuits on null), this is an important edge case that should be verified.

---

### NIT

| # | File | Line | Summary |
|---|------|------|---------|
| 7 | `server/mcp/tools/skills.ts` | 309 | Double-quote string breaks single-quote convention |

**#7 — Double-quote string breaks single-quote convention**
Line 309 uses `"anonymous"` (double quotes) while the broader codebase consistently uses single quotes. Change `|| "anonymous"` to `|| 'anonymous'`.

---

Reviewed SHA: 9dfeacd19e24abbdb2b5295c5f9f58f816adbf6e
